entry.title: paradigm /ˈpærədaɪm/
entry.content:
noun

  • 典范
    He had become the paradigm of the successful man.

  • 范式 a model or example that shows how sth works or is produced; a very clear or typical example of something

entry.title: retrieval /rɪˈtriːvl/
entry.content:
uncountable noun

  • 取回 (formal) the process of getting sth back, especially from a place where it should not be

    The ship was buried, beyond retrieval, at the bottom of the sea.
    (无法挽回)

  • 数据检索 information retrieval

entry.title: derive /dɪˈraɪv/
entry.content:
verb

  • 起源于，来自于
    This word is derived from Latin.

    Our theory was derived from Kruschev's Theory of Darkness.

  • 获得，取得，得到 to get something, especially an advantage or pleasant feeling, from something

    Many students derived enormous satisfaction from the course.

    It is a demanding job, but he derives a lot of satisfaction from it.

    Most of the companies had derived positive benefits from their investment.

  • 从公式推出；从化学方程式获得；文章得出的结论
    We emphasize the need to assume uniformity of extended preferences among individual observes; otherwise, the ordinary utilitarian formula cannot be derives.

    This assumption does not affect the qualitative conclusions derived in the paper.
